titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Fowler Wells Portrait Posters for Physiognomy Lectures
Fowler Wells Portrait Posters for Physiognomy Lectures
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke Staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 12307100.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday April 11, 2018 at 19:27

did.unittitle: Fowler Wells Portrait Posters for Physiognomy Lectures

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: A set of nineteen posters with 112 bust portraits depicting identified men and women, primarily authors, artists, scientists, monarchs, and religious and political leaders from the eighteenth and nineteenth centuries, with a group of idealized heads illustrating characteristics identified by phrenologists.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Fowler & Wells Portrait Posters for Physiognomy Lectures is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Fowler & Wells, Portrait Posters for Physiognomy Lectures. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Fowler & Wells, American practical phrenologists in the mid-nineteenth century, and creators and publishers of illustrative charts, diagrams, and posters for use by lecturers on physiognomy and phrenology. Based in New York, the firm also had offices in Boston, Philadelphia, and London.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A set of nineteen posters with 112 bust portraits depicting identified men and women, primarily authors, artists, scientists, monarchs, and religious and political leaders from the eighteenth and nineteenth centuries, with a group of idealized heads illustrating characteristics identified by phrenologists, such as inhabitiveness and philoprogenitivenes. The portraits, or plates, were rendered in sepia ink and wash on paper, and were mounted on linen or canvas in groups of six; each set has wooden rods at each end to aid in hanging for display, and rolling for compact storage and travel. Each portrait or head is captioned with name of the sitter or the prominent character type portrayed. The posters were likely intended as exhibits supporting phrenology lectures.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Ian Brabner, Bookseller, on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2011.

did.unittitle: Poster 1: Destructiveness small, Destructiveness large, Hydrocephalus James Cardinal, Thomas Sewall, Red Jacket, Language large
Poster 2: William Shakespeare, Alphonse de Lamartine, Alexander von Humboldt, Jonathan Edwards, Perfect Male Head, Perfect Female Head
Poster 3: Johann Gaspar Spurzheim, J. W. Gall, Robert Fulton, Isaac Newton, Constructiveness large, African
Poster 4: George Washington, James Madison, John Adams, James Monroe, Thomas Jefferson, John Quincy Adams
Poster 5: Laura Bridgeman and Oliver Caswell, Junius Brutus, Dioclesian
Poster 6: William Herschel, William Harvey, Charles Sumner, Preston Brooks, Inhabitiveness large, Patrick Henry
Poster 7: Cautiousness small, Aimable-Jean-Jacques Pélissier, John Bunyan, Henry VIII, Omer Pasha, Abdool Medjid (Abdul-Mejid I)
Poster 8: Emanuel Swedenborg, Voltaire, Sarah Josepha Buell Hale, Coarse Organization, Frances Sargent Osgood, Cautiousness large
Poster 9: Washington Irving, Horace Greeley, Aaron Burr, John Knox, Robert R. Livingston, David Hume
Poster 10: Benevolence small, Benevolence large, Robert Pollok, John Collins, Gottfried (a murderer, two portraits)
Poster 11: Moral Sentiments large, Charles Caldwell, Pope Alexander VI, Caligula, Madame de Staël, Joseph C. Neal
Poster 12: Galileo, Firmness large, John Howard, Stephen Decatur, Horatio Nelson, Israel Putnam
Poster 13: Nicolaus Copernicus, Raphael, Elizabeth I, Mary Queen of Scots, Conceited Simpleton, Thief
Poster 14: Prince Gortschakoff, Peter the Great, Frederick William IV, Franz Joseph I, Lord Raglan, Charles Napier
Poster 15: George Washington, Francis Bacon, Alexander Frisbie, Emerson (an idiot), Convict, Tribulation Trepid
Poster 16: Fannie Forrester, William Wordsworth, Jenny Lind, Calculation small, Thomas H. Benton, Harriet Martineau
Poster 17: Martin Luther, Philipp Melanchthon, Alexander Campbell, Oliver Cromwell, Armstrong (a Murderer), Parricide
Poster 18: Socrates, William Tyndale, George Gordon Byron, Alexander Pope, Philoprogenitiveness small, Philoprogenitiveness large
Poster 19: William Cullen Bryant, Benjamin West, Oliver Hazard Perry, Samuel F. B. Morse, Tadeusz Kościuszko, Sydney Smith
